name: Azawakh
description: The Azawakh is a West African sighthound bred by the nomadic Tuareg people of the Sahara and Sahel regions as a guard dog, companion, and coursing hound. They are exceptionally lean and tall with a bone structure visibly beneath their thin skin, yet they are powerful and capable of incredible speed over long distances. Deeply bonded to their families but aloof with strangers, they require experienced owners who understand their independent sighthound temperament.

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4

conditions_detail.name: Wobbler Syndrome (Cervical Spondylomyelopathy)
conditions_detail.description: Wobbler syndrome is spinal cord compression in the neck caused by malformed or unstable vertebrae or herniated discs. It produces a characteristic wobbly gait in the hindlimbs and can progress to complete paralysis.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Medical management with strict rest, anti-inflammatories, and physiotherapy is used for mild cases. Surgical decompression and stabilisation is recommended for moderate to severe neurological deficits.
prevention_tips: Avoid rapid growth in predisposed breeds
